nts1.n: Circuits w/ Olly Chubb: Chain Reaction Special
nts1.d: Connections in electronic music: the history of house and techno as presented to you by the wonderful Olly Chubb.

nts1.n: A Fist in the Face of God presents: Midnite Madness CCXII - Eternal Death
nts1.d: Midnite Madness endeavours to unearth the spirit of the ancient metal gods as well as the genre's new flesh, taking in most styles—heavy, thrash, black, speed, death, punk, doom, etc. There will be some exclusion: no nu-metal, no triggered drums, no posers and no requests. Only metal is real!

nts1.n: NTS X STRATA-EAST RECORDS SPECIAL (MIXED BY JEAN-CLAUDE) (R)
ORGANIC MUSIC TOKYO W/ CHEE SHIMIZU
THE EARLY BIRD SHOW W/ SPIRIT BLUE
THE NTS BREAKFAST SHOW W/ FLO
SOUP TO NUTS W/ SHY ONE
A LONG STRANGE DREAM W/ BATU

nts2.d: French Cambodian audio-visual artist Neo Geodesia shares 60 minutes of traditional Cambodian martial arts music “for the chaotic modern era”, inspired by new release “Okhna Stamina”.

Optimo is JG Wilkes & JD Twitch; record label, and longstanding Glaswegian party. You can expect a lot from their new monthly slot, sure to dig up some obscurities from post-punk, new wave, EBM, house and techno. Bags of unreleased stuff, too.

nts2.n: Blooming In Filth w/ CRISIS ACTING (R)
Amarillo on Everything w/ Hayden Pedigo

subtle.n: In The Loop w/ Sam Groves

subtle.d: With a monthly show on Subtle, Niceup is a London based DJ with a unique style. Pushing UKG, Breakbeat, Bass, Electro and 140. Weaving through genres showcasing new releases and older cuts.

Jedah started DJ'ing at the tender age of 18 years of age specialising in UK Garage/Grime. 

Jedah is currently the DJ for Grime Original Hollowman Jendor of the former Grime Collective Essentials/ OG's.

Responsible for “that massive grime tune with the Psycho strings”, UK producers/DJs Trends & Boylan are well versed in what the scene has to offer. They use this platform to showcase music from across the 140 spectrum from Grime to Dubstep. Anything goes!!

subtle.n: Out of Phase With Sam Ling
Gimme Signal radio w/ Kim Öhman

subtle.d: Sessions w/ Amy B is an underground multi-genre music show, showcasing the most prolific guests from all corners of the music industry.

Originally linking up in Wellington and playing shows with the Breaking Beats crew, Swampman and Zero Crumbs have reunited – bringing their smooth mixing and flexible selections to East London. 

In their monthly show, the pair explore the deep, dark, and soulful ends of DNB. Expect fresh underground tunes, deep cut classics, and a bit of banter in Kiwi accents.

subtle.n: Dun Roamin w/ Tomi From Brockley

subtle.d:
Midlands born, London based producer and DJ influenced by UK funky, broken techno, garage and early dubstep. 

Has released on Keysound recordings, RKS, eatmybeat, ec2a, Durkle Disco and Dimsum records

2Step Selects is a monthly show hosted by the Choki Biki Records crew. Founded in Dublin, Ireland, CBR consists of DJ Sharpson, 2step.png, Frame Shift, and MICK. The show consists of an all round breaks, electro, ukg and jungle hour, followed by an Irish showcase, demonstrating all the best Ireland has to offer collective/party/Dj & Producer wise!

Saraphim (aka RNS & RDubz) are two Essex based Producer/DJ’s currently focused on Dubstep & the 140 spectrum, mixing ethnical and grime influences into their music. You can expect anything from deep and melodic meditative vibes to high energy driven music.
London based DJ who enjoys exploring the darker, bassier corners of Jungle, Breaks, Techno and Drum & Bass, especially when it is laced with a bit of acid.

subtle.n: The Ice Box Sessions with Frigid

dublab.n: Echo Park Film Center - Optical Track
RERUN: Cafe Lounge – 2025 Membership Drive Finale

dublab.d: “Dying Songs focuses on melancholic electronic music but isn’t limited to that. Sometimes it gets dark, sometimes I try to mix techno awkwardly, sometimes i get nostalgic… I like stuff that’s dreamy, ghostly, pretty and sad. I’ll try out new productions of my own, as well. I’m keeping it pretty open and we’ll see how it evolves.” – Jimmy

dublab.n: Digital Drums OR NO ONE TURNED AWAY
Daddy Differently and Dirty Dave - Things of Life
dublab.d: It’s the simple things isn’t it? The little things of life. Good food, sunshine, no traffic, dark coffee, 72˚ breeze, telephone off the hook and music every Friday morning a la Daddy Differently & Dirty Dave.
Peak Oil records presents ‘tipping point’; a monthly journey through the record collections of brian and brion. Leaving no good genre unturned, enjoy two hours of esoterica, conversation and guests.

dublab.n: Ill Camille x Jesse Fairfax - The Sweeter the Juice
Greg Belson - Divine Chord Gospel Show
Gabie Strong and Ian Wellman - Zone Mapping

rinsefr.n: La Passe D avec Armel Bizzman

sutro.n: DYATRON INDUSTRIES - let's go all in, to go all out!
